scope: Maintenance tracker structure and root index
acceptance: Tracker files exist, the maintenance root index points to them, and normal goal-mode work can start from overview.md and roadmap.csv.
verification: docs/maintenance/core-modernization exists with overview.md, roadmap.csv, capability-ledger.csv, dependency-ledger.csv, progress.md, and checkpoints/*.csv; docs/maintenance/README.md points to the tracker.
notes: Use continuous CM-001 numbering to avoid unexplained checkpoint gaps.

acceptance: The tracker states that mature libraries replace obsolete native owners where suitable, that native fallback backends must not remain, and that ED2K stays native unless a real maintained embeddable library is identified.
verification: overview.md records the goal contract, library policy, removal policy, 99 percent policy, and test policy; ledgers record initial target ownership.
notes: This intentionally rejects compatibility-preserving dual backends.

acceptance: Large remaining native subsystems are mapped before deletion begins.
verification: The source inventory is recorded in progress.md and capability-ledger.csv; local inventory found HTTP/RPC/WebSocket 70 files, FTP/SFTP/SSH 25, network runtime 53, Metalink/XML 47, disk/piece 58, crypto/compression 49, core orchestration 51, ED2K 48, and libtorrent boundary 25.
notes: This inventory is a planning baseline, not a deletion list.

scope: Prior maintenance work that this tracker must respect
acceptance: The tracker references the completed libtorrent, ED2K reference-alignment, and ED2K hardening baselines without rewriting their history.
verification: overview.md and progress.md identify libtorrent-bt-migration, ed2k-refactor, and ed2k-download-hardening as baseline trackers.
notes: Use earlier trackers as source of truth for BT and ED2K boundaries.

acceptance: All tracker CSV files parse and Markdown has no obvious formatting breakage.
verification: CSV parser check passed for 22 files; git diff --check passed for docs/maintenance/README.md and docs/maintenance/core-modernization.
notes: No implementation code changes belong in CM-001.
